tokens: The mission has split in two with separate control teams in Houston.
This transcript is parallel to the chapter, "Day 6, part 1: Waking in the descent orbit", but only contains the communication between the CSM CapCom Ken Mattingly and Command Module Pilot Ron Evans.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The audio recordings presented in this chapter were acquired from the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA).
It appears that long quiet periods have been deleted, probably by a process of copying from one tape machine to another.
For this reason, indications of pauses in utterance will be omitted here.
Except to split the original three-hour archive files into smaller chunks, the recordings have not been further edited for the journal.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I finally got my suit on.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I'm - haven't - changed the canister yet.
Let me check and see if I can have time to do that or not.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Clanking sounds can be heard on the voice downlink as Ron works with the metalwork of the probe and drogue.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I'm Simplex Alpha and VHF, Right.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I don't read you at all, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: No, I don't read you at all either, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I've got everything on in my Auto panel.
Let me try the other one for backup.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You were kind of clipping there for a little bit.
I got the last part of your transmission.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I heard that it was just - I missed the just - It was just warming up, but I got the loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I read you loud and clear; that's VHF B.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Yeah, I didn't hear you at all that time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: "How do you read now?" I got that.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: [Laughter] Then I missed that part of it.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: More metallic clanking noises in the CM.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Can you move your mike a little bit closer or something?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: No, I didn't read you at all that time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Well, you're going to have to yell or something.
I still - you're still clipping yourself out, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: No, I didn't read you that time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Bravo is Off; Simplex Alpha is On.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I don't read you guys at all, except through the tunnel.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Got you loud and clear that time.
And, Gene, can you verify the capture latches are all engaged?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I didn't read you at all, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I got you that time, and check the capture latches.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: In the following audio clip, there is no utterance from Ron for the first minute.
However, this audio is being presented because it includes much metallic clanking as Ron works to reinstall the docking equipment.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Yeah, I'm up in the tunnel, but go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I'm getting the probe umbilicals installed now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Can you look at the capture latches?
I've got it preloaded here now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Preload the - it's pushing on by, so I'll going to - [garble] preloaded.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: What do you mean; probe locked?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Wait a minute; I don't have SCS.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Gene has just requested that Ron give him the values in the computer's memory knownas Noun 20.
These are the angles of the three gimbals in the IMU that represent the CSM's current attitude with respect to the current reference (the REFSMMAT).
Gene will adjust these to account for the different orientations of the two spacecraft coordinate systems.
The resulting angles will then be used for an initial coarse alignment of the LM's IMU.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I don't know how it got off attitude.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Yeah, I was going to ask you the same thing.
You might have knocked a - a stick or something while everybody was flailing around down there.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Maybe - maybe I hit the stick or something.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: We're Min Deadband, Att Hold at 0105 and 0.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I forgot to release the docking latches.
I'll go up and release docking latches 1 and 7.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And if I just put the - No, I'll do that.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I was going to say you could read the probe temp if I put the circuit breakers in.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: One releases; two releases, and it's free...
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: One release, two releases, and it's free.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Docking Probe circuit breakers; two of them are going Close.
Probe Extend/Release is going to Retract.
Ah ha, I've got two barber poles!
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Docking probe circuit breakers are going Open.
Extend/Releases, and they went gray, of course, when then went open.
Verify probe extend latch engage indicator's not visible.
Well, I'll go back - it wasn't a while ago, but I'll go look again.
Only if you hit Extend with the circuit breakers in.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: With Ron having slightly changed the attitude of the stack a few minutes ago to restore the planned undocking attitude, Gene wants to obtain a fresh coarse alignment of the LM's IMU.
This is again based on the IMU in the Command Module but with appropriate conversion to the LM's orientation.
Again, this is by Ron selecting Verb 6 Noun 20.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Gene counts to a Mark for Ron to get the gimbal angles.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: America, Houston, in order to get on and stay on the time line, I'll remind you about the lithium canister after PDI; we can let that one go.
And if you'd like to let the camera business slide until after your suit check and we'll get those things.
I'll come back and remind you of those, too.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I've got the - the cameras all set.
I don't have the - the right values on the lenses yet; but, anyhow, the camera are out and they're loaded.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I've got mag OO on the Hasselblad.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Why don't I go ahead and get the PGA ver out of the way, and then we can get some of the - data, or would you rather get the data up first?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Why don't we go ahead and get the PGA, and I'll just sit on these PADs, and could you tell me if you ever got around to playing with the squelch on the VHF?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, let's see, we're at Squelch A now.
I've even got it all the way down to 1, and I still don't hear them.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You did get a satisfactory check on your side, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I got a satisfactory - Every once in a while I can hear them now; they're cutting in and out somewhere, I don't know where.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Squelch A is sitting at about 4.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And, America, we'll give you a state vector if you give us Accept.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The next audio clip includes about fifty seconds of cabin noise before Ron speaks.
He is on VOX (voice-operated switch) but with the sensitivity turned up so that his mike is always live to Earth.
There are sounds that may be a camera with an electric motorwind being operated or perhaps a gas valve being opened and closed.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: How much time to AOS, yet - LOS?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Got a little time for the PGA ver then, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And it's your computer, anytime you want to get to it, Ron.

tokens: Ah ha, I got the old helmet and gloves on!
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: More cables all over the place here than I know what to do with.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Well, this place looks like a pigpen - you've run the same course everyone else has, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: [Laughter] I got cables and hoses and...
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Son-of-a-muck - I hit the stick again.
Now I got to unlock it.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You want me to maneuver back to attitude, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: We're out of attitude just a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Well I'm Min Deadband, but I maneuvered out of attitude.
You want to go back to regular attitude?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: The other two are closed; mine is open, and I got the inner connect in between the other ones.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: That's going up to Press, and it feels like it's going up.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And terminal Direct O2on air to [garble] O2Flow High.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I'll just go to Receive on the VHF.
I'll just go to Receive on the VHF, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Let's see, and there's a Delta-P of about - 6½ - Now we're going to cycle suit circuit return here.
Now we can squirt a little more O2flow in.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Looks like about 4 on our data, Ron.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Let's say about maybe nine or something like that.
Can't tell for sure what it feels like.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: We're - we're showing 4.2 to 4.3 on our pressure spread, and you're cabin and suit pressure gauges probably show something similar.
And we show that the flow rate is down.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And the O2flow is staying pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: [Garble] let's go to Depress on this thing, huh?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay; so it looks like it's sta - stable there, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And my ears are pretty good.
If you guys are okay I'm going to go to Off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: America, I don't know if I copied you correctly.
You don't want to go to Off on that Suit Test valve until the pressures are back down to normal.
If that was what you asked.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: My ears are popping to meet demand - I changed my mind, I'll let it go down slowly.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And we've got about 20 minutes before LOS, so there's no hurry on those PADs.
I've got them standing by when you're ready, and didn't know what you - what you had in mind about working on these things.

We might get - you might get started on the hatch integrity check, if you get to that point, and I can give you the PADs while you're waiting for it to bleed down or whatever it turns out to be convenient.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Let's see, I guess it's about time to - Integrity check; okay.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And I guess the LM is going to be asking you for a Noun 20 pretty soon like, here.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Gene gives Ron another Mark when he should recall the CSM's gimbal angles on the DSKY.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And Houston, do we want to release those docking latches before they do their hot fire?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You're right, Ron, we'll - we'll just hold up on that release on the latches until the hot fires are over.
So, when you get your copying hand ready, why, we'll give you some PADs.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Let's see - where are we going to start on the PADs?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And you might go to Block on the computer, too.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The first thing I'll give you is the Sep PAD.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Roll is 0, 105 pitch, and yaw is 0.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Noun 33, 110:27:55.00, and roll, pitch, and yaw are as depicted: 0, 105, and 0.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: The next thing I have for you is a P24 PAD that goes on page 115.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Suit Test valves in Off now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, Wide Deadband Att/Hold, that's what I'm on.
And Amer - Houston; America, I missed T-2 on.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Are you ready to go back to it now?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll read back what I have.
TCA is 01:00; T-3 is 01:26; Roll 15, pitch 297, and 0; North 02.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Explanation of P24 PAD follows:Target:17-1, which is a landmark at the planned landing site.T-1 (110:58:13 GET)is the time that the landmark is on the horizon and is 2 minutes, 47 seconds before closest approach.T-2 (111:00:30)is 2 minute, 17 seconds after T1 and is when Ron begins to pitch the spacecraft at 2° per second to keep it at an approximately constant attitude with respect to the landmark.TCA (111:01:00)is the time of closest approach.T-3 (111:01:26)is 3 minutes, 13 seconds after T1 and 26 seconds after TCA.

It marks the end of the landmark tracking mode.Spacecraft attitude at T-2:x, 15°; y, 297°; z, 0°.An additional note states that at TCA, the spacecraft will pass 2 nautical miles (3.7 km) north of the landmark.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Challenger said something every time you talked on that one.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Hey, Jack, could you go to Receive only on the VHF for a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You can go ahead on the next PAD.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: On page 113, I've got a DAP load for you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, CSM weight is 37983; pak to off, plus 0.40; yak to off, plus 0.91.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And the last one comes up on page 121, and it's a LM P76.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The hot fire - you want Free, Challenger?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You want Free for the hot fire?
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You can try that one again.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Noun 33 is 112:02:51.92; minus 0007.5 and all zips for Y and Z.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're cut out on Noun 33 again, and I've got minus 0007.5 and X, Y, and Z are 0.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: You didn't get very far off that time, either.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I'm going to turn off B3 and also my roll jets, and then I'm going to undo the docking latches.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, Ron, we're about 5 minutes from LOS.
And the only thing that you wouldn't be able to do following right down your checklist is to get the VHF data on and check the tape recorder at LOS.
The rest of that stuff you can follow right on through, and we'll remind you on the lithium change after descent.
So it looks like we're caught up again.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The two spacecraft are being held together by twelve strong docking latches arranged around the docking ring, and by three small capture latches on the tip of the probe now engaged in the LM's drogue.
The capture latches have been preloaded so that as Ron releases the docking latches, the seal between the ships should be maintained.
The capture latches do not have the strength of the docking latches and so to avoid undue stress on them, Ron has disabled some of the CSM's RCS thrusters.
The possibility of an unexpected break in the seal is why Ron is suited and why he is asking Mission Control to keep an eye on the flow of oxygen into the cabin.
Should there be an escape of air through a compromised seal, the spacecraft would increase the flow of O2to compensate.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Number 1 is disconnected; and, Houston, could you watch my O2flow - as I disconnect these things?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: One , two - and it's fully released, and the hook is off of the docking ring.
Number 6 is - one, two - and it's fully released, and the hook is off of the docking ring.
Okay, 7 is released, and the hook is off of the docking ring.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: And the chute's fully released, and the hook is off the docking ring.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: One, two, and she's fully released; the hook clears the docking ring.
Here's one, two - fully released, and the hook clears the docking ring.
Okay, handle's free; J-hook clears the ring.
I've only got one more to go.
There's the first latch there's two latches [garble] came over.
And the hook stays clear; the handle is free.
And, by golly, they're all off.
You're hanging on those three little bitty things.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I put the old - hatch in.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, Ron, we're within a minute of LOS.
Your O2flow still looks good, and the rest of the stuff that you can get on the VHF is going to be just fine.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll get that VHF stuff and then put the hatch in.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Okay, we're in Receive Only to B Data.
